task_completion.description: Create a rectangular sketch for the base plate.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 2D rectangle drawn in the SolveSpace canvas. Look for dimension labels of 50 and 30 if visible.

task_completion.description: Extrude the rectangular sketch into a 10mm thick 3D plate.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 2D rectangle is transformed into a solid 3D block. The property browser or text window may show an extrusion depth of 10.

task_completion.description: Create a centered circular sketch for the hole.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 2D circle drawn on the face of the 3D plate, positioned in the center. Look for a diameter dimension label of 12.

task_completion.description: Perform a boolean difference operation to create the through-hole.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A visible hole cutting completely through the 3D plate. The SolveSpace property browser or group list should indicate a 'Difference' or boolean cut operation.

task_completion.description: Save the model to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog showing the path '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/mounting_plate.slvs', or the SolveSpace window title bar displaying 'mounting_plate.slvs'.

integrity.description: The agent used the SolveSpace GUI to create the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking tools, drawing sketches, and navigating the SolveSpace graphical interface, rather than writing a .slvs text file in a terminal or code editor.

integrity.description: The hole is a genuine 3D boolean cut, not just a 2D circle drawn on top.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final 3D view shows depth and empty space inside the hole, confirming it is a through-hole and not just a surface sketch.

integrity.description: The agent built the model from scratch.
integrity.visual_evidence: Sequential screenshots show the progression from an empty canvas to sketches, extrusions, and boolean operations, rather than simply opening a pre-existing completed file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is exactly '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/mounting_plate.slvs'. The base rectangle should have dimensions 50mm x 30mm, extruded to 10mm thickness. The hole must be 12mm in diameter and created using a boolean difference operation (look for 'Difference' in the SolveSpace property browser or group list). The verifier primarily checks for the presence of structural CAD operations (extrusion, boolean difference, sketch groups).
